paragraphs: Martim Almeida is a Portuguese concert pianist whose work moves fluidly between the solo repertoire and the chamber stage. A prizewinner at the Porto International Piano Competition and a former participant in the Leeds International Piano Competition, he has appeared in recital across Portugal, Spain, France, the Netherlands and the United Kingdom.

His repertoire centres on the Romantic and late-Romantic literature — Schubert, Brahms, Liszt and the Portuguese composers who studied with them — but extends back to Beethoven's late sonatas and forward to the impressionist writing of Debussy and Freitas Branco.

A keen chamber musician, Martim is a founding member of the Almeida–Lopes–Carvalho Trio, with whom he has recorded the complete Brahms clarinet trios for Hyperion. His solo disc for Aparté — Schubert's late sonatas — was named Editor's Choice by Gramophone and received the Prémio Pessoa for classical recording in 2024.

Martim studied at the Escola Superior de Música e das Artes do Espectáculo in Porto, the Conservatoire National Supérieur in Paris and the Royal Academy of Music in London. His principal teachers were Sofia Mendes, Bruno Rigutto and Christopher Elton.
